title: 7.3.4 Evaluation Procedures for In-Plane Behavior of Perforated Walls with Spandrel Damage
description: Procedures for estimating the moment and shear capacity of uncracked spandrels and damaged walls experiencing joint sliding or unit cracking.

moment_capacity_Mspun.methodology: Derived from interlock between bed joints and collar joints at the pier-spandrel interface. Assumes elastic stress distribution with neutral axis at centerline of spandrel height.

variables.d_effun: Distance between Resultant Tensile (T) and Compressive (C) forces, calculated as (2/3) * d_sp
variables.T: Resultant tensile force, calculated as T = [(v_bjun * b_w * b_effun) + (v_cun * b_h * b_effun * (NB - 1)) * eta]

shear_capacity_Vspun.methodology: Derived from diagonal tensile capacity for the pier.
shear_capacity_Vspun.formula: V_spun = f'_dt * d_sp * b_sp * beta * (1 + f_ae / f'_dt)^0.5

variables.beta: 0.67 for L_sp/d_sp < 0.67; 1.0 for L_sp/d_sp > 1.0; interpolated between
variables.f_ae: Expected horizontal axial stress in the pier (assumed 0 unless known)

moment_capacity_Mspcr.methodology: Similar to uncracked spandrel but assumes bond strength of mortar (epsilon) is lost.

variables.v_bjcr: Cracked bed joint shear stress = 0.25 * P_CE / A_n
variables.b_effcr: Effective length of interface = b_l/2 - Delta_s
variables.Delta_s: Average slip (estimated as average opening width of open head joint)

cracked_spandrel_joint_sliding.shear_capacity_logic: Assumed same as uncracked spandrel provided keying action remains; resisted by bearing on bed joints of interlocked units.

cracked_spandrel_unit_cracking.methodology: Derived similar to uncracked spandrel procedures, but effective depth of the spandrel is reduced to the amount of uncracked masonry remaining.

implications_of_spandrel_cracking.corner_damage: Caused by weight of masonry near the joint. Exceeding weak capacities leads to diagonal cracks propagating from opening corners across the last pier, increasing its effective height.

multistory_pier_rocking.logic: If spandrels are fully cracked, they provide no bending rigidity. Pier rocking must be assessed using a multistory pier model.
multistory_pier_rocking.governing_mode: To determine the governing mode, compare values for V_rR for multi-story and single-story rocking. The lowest capacity governs.

implications_of_spandrel_cracking.reference_diagram: Figure 7-14 illustrates implications of spandrel cracking including Mechanism 1 and Mechanism 2.
